headline: Hewlett Packard Enterprise stock soars 36% as AI server demand drives FY outlook
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) posted record second-quarter results on Monday and accelerated its long-term financial goals by two years as expansion of AI data centers boosts demand for its servers and networking products, sending its shares soaring 36% during extended hours of trading.

headline: Analyst Says Dell’s Momentum Is Real, But Valuation Is Risky with Stock Up 250% YTD
summary: Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) has become one of the market’s biggest AI winners. The stock has rocketed over 250% year to date and more than 100% in the past month alone, fueled by explosive demand for AI infrastructure. headline: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) Soars on Record Q2 Beat and Sky-High Guidance
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise smashed Q2 estimates with record revenue of $10.7B, surging 40% YoY. Driven by AI and networking demand, HPE raised full-year guidance well above expectations, sending its stock up over 30% in after-hours trading.

headline: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) Surprises With Strong Q1 CY2026, Stock Jumps 28.5%
summary: Enterprise technology company Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) reported Q1 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, with sales up 40% year on year to $10.68 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($11.8 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 8.7% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.79 per share was 47.8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: HPE Names Chris Hsu to Board of Directors
summary: HOUSTON, June 01, 2026--HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced the appointment of Christopher (Chris) Hsu, a Partner at Elliott Investment Management L.P., to its Board of Directors, effective immediately. Hsu will join the Board's Strategy Committee, as well as its Finance & Investment Committee.

headline: HPE fast forwards to 2028 goals on robust AI demand, shares surge 36%
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise posted record second-quarter results on Monday, prompting it to accelerate its long-term financial goals by two years, as expansion ‌of AI data centers boosts demand, sending its shares up 36% in extended trading.  HPE, ‌which competes with Dell and Super Micro Computer, is benefiting as customers increasingly buy its servers and networking products ​to power AI applications such as ChatGPT.  U.S. tech giants including Alphabet and Amazon plan to spend over $700 billion on AI infrastructure this year, which would bolster demand for suppliers such as HPE.

HPE, ‌which competes with Dell and Super Micro Computer, is benefiting as customers increasingly buy its servers and networking products ​to power AI applications such as ChatGPT.  U.S. tech giants including Alphabet and Amazon plan to spend over $700 billion on AI infrastructure this year, which would bolster demand for suppliers such as HPE.

headline: HPE Earnings Beat. It’s Another AI Beneficiary.
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise  reported better-than-expected financial results and raised its fiscal year outlook as artificial intelligence demand continues to benefit the company’s growth.  HPE’s  earnings follow strong financial results from peer Dell Technologies, proving yet again that demand for servers is strong as customers buy up the hardware needed to power AI.  Dell reported much better-than-expected financial results for its first-quarter and boosted its revenue outlook for the fiscal year on May 28.

headline: Software Strikes Back - Dell Detonates, Abel Finally Spends, Nvidia Aims at the PC
summary: A computer maker most investors had filed under "boring hardware" jumped a third in one session. The Dow crossed 51,000 for the first time. And over the weekend, Greg Abel finally did the thing the market had been waiting for, he wrote an $8.5 billion cheque. Three storylines, three different signals, all worth pulling apart before Monday's open.

headline: Hewlett Packard, Science Applications And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Monday
summary: Stocks to watch: HPE, SAIC, CRDO, NVDA, SMCI. HPE and SMCI report earnings. Nvidia's CEO speaks at tradeshow
